title: espeletia brassicoidea subsp. angusta
intro: Espeletia brassicoidea subsp. angusta, also known as the Angustifolia or Narrow-leaved Espenletia, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region in South America. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Espeletia brassicoidea subsp. angusta is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
